sourceMovie.title: Hush
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Hush (2016) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Mike Flanagan's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Don't Breathe
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hush clicked for you, Don't Breathe (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hush?
faq.answer: Try Don't Breathe, The Invisible Man, Halloween—each shares DNA with Hush while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hush?
faq.answer: Hush (2016) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hush worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
